edges.description: Impaired proximal tubular reabsorption produces characteristic low-molecular-weight proteinuria.
Megalin-dependent sensory tissue dysfunction contributes to hearing loss.
Megalin dysfunction across ocular tissues contributes to characteristic eye abnormalities.
Ocular megalin dysfunction contributes to severe myopia.
High myopia and retinal involvement increase retinal detachment risk.
Megalin-dependent developmental signaling disruption contributes to corpus callosum malformation.
Brain developmental effects of LRP2 deficiency contribute to intellectual disability.
Embryonic megalin pathway disruption contributes to structural congenital malformations.
Embryonic megalin pathway disruption contributes to abdominal wall congenital malformations.
Multiorgan developmental dysfunction contributes to epilepsy in Donnai-Barrow syndrome.
Multiorgan developmental dysfunction contributes to neurodevelopmental impairment.
Multiorgan developmental dysfunction can contribute to autistic behavioral features.
Embryonic megalin pathway disruption contributes to the facio-oculo-acoustico-renal craniofacial presentation.

nodes.description: Facial dysmorphism is part of the classic facio-oculo-acoustico-renal syndrome presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral Phenotype, Electroclinical Features, and Treatment Options in Twins with Lrp2 Candidate Variants (Donnay-Barrow/Foar Syndrome).
evidence.snippet: LRP2 is implicated in an autosomal recessive disorder characterized by dimorphisms, ocular anomalies, sensorineural deafness, proteinuria, epilepsy, and intellectual disability: a clinical condition called Donnai-Barrow syndrome (DBS) or facio-oculo-acoustico-renal (FOAR) syndrome.

nodes.description: Agenesis or hypogenesis of the corpus callosum is a common structural brain feature.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Additional common features include agenesis of the corpus callosum, sensorineural hearing loss, intellectual disability, and congenital diaphragmatic hernia and/or omphalocele.

nodes.description: Antiseizure medications are used for seizure management in affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral Phenotype, Electroclinical Features, and Treatment Options in Twins with Lrp2 Candidate Variants (Donnay-Barrow/Foar Syndrome).
evidence.snippet: The patients were under treatment with risperidone, antiseizure medications (ASMs), and supplementation of alpha-lactalbumin for self-injury and sleep disturbance.

nodes.description: Autistic features are reported in affected individuals with Donnai-Barrow syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral Phenotype, Electroclinical Features, and Treatment Options in Twins with Lrp2 Candidate Variants (Donnay-Barrow/Foar Syndrome).
evidence.snippet: We provide a clinical report of two mono-chorionic twins with LRP2-related disease manifesting developmental delay, autistic features, seizures, proteinuria, and sleep disorders.

nodes.description: Cochlear implantation may be considered for severe or profound sensorineural hearing loss.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Surgical repair of diaphragmatic hernia and/or omphalocele; corrective lenses for myopia; preventive treatments for retinal detachment; hearing aids and/or cochlear implants for hearing loss; antiepileptic drugs for seizures; supplementation as needed for low serum vitamins A and D; education tailored to degree of intellectual, visual, and hearing abilities.

nodes.description: Congenital diaphragmatic hernia is a recurrent structural malformation in Donnai-Barrow syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Additional common features include agenesis of the corpus callosum, sensorineural hearing loss, intellectual disability, and congenital diaphragmatic hernia and/or omphalocele.

nodes.description: Developmental monitoring, educational planning, and support should be tailored to intellectual, visual, and hearing abilities.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Surgical repair of diaphragmatic hernia and/or omphalocele; corrective lenses for myopia; preventive treatments for retinal detachment; hearing aids and/or cochlear implants for hearing loss; antiepileptic drugs for seizures; supplementation as needed for low serum vitamins A and D; education tailored to degree of intellectual, visual, and hearing abilities.

nodes.description: Developmental delay is a recurrent neurodevelopmental manifestation of Donnai-Barrow syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral Phenotype, Electroclinical Features, and Treatment Options in Twins with Lrp2 Candidate Variants (Donnay-Barrow/Foar Syndrome).
evidence.snippet: We provide a clinical report of two mono-chorionic twins with LRP2-related disease manifesting developmental delay, autistic features, seizures, proteinuria, and sleep disorders.

nodes.description: Hearing aids and cochlear implantation are used for hearing loss management when clinically appropriate.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Surgical repair of diaphragmatic hernia and/or omphalocele; corrective lenses for myopia; preventive treatments for retinal detachment; hearing aids and/or cochlear implants for hearing loss; antiepileptic drugs for seizures; supplementation as needed for low serum vitamins A and D; education tailored to degree of intellectual, visual, and hearing abilities.

nodes.description: Severe myopia is a characteristic ocular complication of Donnai-Barrow syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Ocular complications include high myopia, retinal detachment, retinal dystrophy, and progressive vision loss.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability is part of the neurodevelopmental phenotype, distinct from early developmental delay.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Additional common features include agenesis of the corpus callosum, sensorineural hearing loss, intellectual disability, and congenital diaphragmatic hernia and/or omphalocele.

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral Phenotype, Electroclinical Features, and Treatment Options in Twins with Lrp2 Candidate Variants (Donnay-Barrow/Foar Syndrome).
evidence.snippet: By sequencing clinical exome, LRP2 candidate rare variants, c.6815G > A, p. (Arg2272His), inherited from the mother and c.12725A > G, p. (Asp4242Gly), inherited from the father, were identified.

nodes.description: Donnai-Barrow syndrome is caused by LRP2 deficiency. Loss of megalin disrupts multiligand receptor-mediated endocytosis across affected organs, including proximal renal tubule and sensory tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Cryo-EM structures elucidate the multiligand receptor nature of megalin.
evidence.snippet: Genetic megalin deficiency causes Donnai-Barrow syndrome/facio-oculo-acoustico-renal syndrome in humans.

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral Phenotype, Electroclinical Features, and Treatment Options in Twins with Lrp2 Candidate Variants (Donnay-Barrow/Foar Syndrome).
evidence.snippet: The LRP2 gene encodes megalin (LRP-2/GP330), a large single-spanning transmembrane glycoprotein that serves as a multiligand endocytotic receptor and mediates the reabsorption of albumin in the proximal renal tubule.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in LRP2, which encodes the multiligand receptor megalin, cause Donnai-Barrow and facio-oculo-acoustico-renal syndromes.
evidence.snippet: LRP2 encodes megalin, a multiligand uptake receptor that regulates levels of diverse circulating compounds.

nodes.description: Ocular anomalies are a defining component of the FOAR syndrome phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral Phenotype, Electroclinical Features, and Treatment Options in Twins with Lrp2 Candidate Variants (Donnay-Barrow/Foar Syndrome).
evidence.snippet: LRP2 is implicated in an autosomal recessive disorder characterized by dimorphisms, ocular anomalies, sensorineural deafness, proteinuria, epilepsy, and intellectual disability: a clinical condition called Donnai-Barrow syndrome (DBS) or facio-oculo-acoustico-renal (FOAR) syndrome.

nodes.description: Omphalocele or related umbilical hernia is a recurrent abdominal wall malformation.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Additional common features include agenesis of the corpus callosum, sensorineural hearing loss, intellectual disability, and congenital diaphragmatic hernia and/or omphalocele.

nodes.description: Ophthalmologic monitoring, corrective lenses for myopia, and preventive retinal detachment treatment are key eye-management measures.

nodes.description: Persistent tubular proteinuria is a characteristic renal feature of Donnai-Barrow syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Behavioral Phenotype, Electroclinical Features, and Treatment Options in Twins with Lrp2 Candidate Variants (Donnay-Barrow/Foar Syndrome).
evidence.snippet: LRP2 is implicated in an autosomal recessive disorder characterized by dimorphisms, ocular anomalies, sensorineural deafness, proteinuria, epilepsy, and intellectual disability: a clinical condition called Donnai-Barrow syndrome (DBS) or facio-oculo-acoustico-renal (FOAR) syndrome.

nodes.description: Kidney function, urinalysis, and serum vitamins A and D should be monitored because megalin dysfunction causes low-molecular-weight proteinuria.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Ophthalmologic surveillance to monitor for retinal detachment; serial audiologic examinations; serial measurement of kidney function including blood urea nitrogen and serum creatinine concentrations, urinalysis, and serum vitamin A and D; monitor developmental progress and educational needs.

nodes.description: Retinal detachment is a clinically important ocular complication and surveillance target.

evidence.reference_title: Donnai-Barrow Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Ocular complications include high myopia, retinal detachment, retinal dystrophy, and progressive vision loss.

nodes.description: Seizures are part of the neurologic phenotype in Donnai-Barrow syndrome.
